messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Enhance your statistical toolkit with advanced modeling techniques for complex observational data. Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 11 with Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). Epidemiological researchers can apply these advanced methods to model complex, non-additive exposure-outcome pathways and latent health states more accurately. By integrating nonlinear structural equation modeling into your R workflow, you can better estimate threshold effects, latent interactions, and curvilinear growth trajectories in longitudinal cohort data. This allows for more robust causal mediation analyses and realistic measurement models when analyzing observational health datasets.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/nonlinear-structural-equation-modeling) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Overview of the GDIS Hub
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Enhance your epidemiological research by integrating robust, disaggregated statistical data into your analytical workflows. Overview of the GDIS Hub (Free Seminar) is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 23, led by Fran?ois Marshall (Statistics Canada). This upcoming seminar introduces the Gender, Diversity and Inclusion Statistics (GDIS) Hub as a major entry point for disaggregated and intersectional statistical evidence. Epidemiologists can leverage these robust, high-quality public data resources to move beyond aggregate summaries and illuminate health heterogeneity across intersecting social categories. By mastering the Hub's structure and data holdings, you can integrate official statistics directly into your R-based epidemiological models to improve the precision of your empirical claims and strengthen peer-reviewed publications.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/overview-of-the-gdis-hub-free-seminar) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
